_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify and document customer requirements when on-boarding new data assets.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting data consumer discovery, translating consumer data needs into data product requirements with defined quality, freshness, and access standards.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-S-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-008 (DQ, cosine: 0.544), DG-S-008 (DG, cosine: 0.529).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.618). Shared vocabulary: [data, requirements]. DCWF-only terms [assets, boarding, customer, document, identify, new] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, conducting, consumer, defined, discovery, freshness] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.883 (P=0.892, R=0.875). Balanced P/R — the 10-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Identify and document customer requirements when on-boarding new data assets." and "Skill in conducting data consumer discovery, translating consumer data needs int" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.562 < STS 0.618 (gap -0.056). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [assets, boarding, customer] and [access, conducting, consumer] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.5%. Shared tokens: [data, requirements]. DCWF-only: [assets, boarding, customer, document, identify, new]. WIDAI-only: [access, conducting, consumer, defined, discovery, freshness].
